title: Astronauts Make History in Space

title: Moon Shakes: Our Shrinking Lunar Neighbor
story: Crackle and pop! Our moon is getting smaller. A study published in 2019 revealed that the moon is still cooling down and shrinking, causing moonquakes along the way. Scientists discovered this by examining data from special instruments left on the lunar surface. As the moon's core cools, its crust wrinkles like a raisin, creating cliffs and cracks where moonquakes happen. This exciting news, shared on May 13, shows that Earth's closest neighbor is more active than we thought!
activity_question: What is causing the moon to shrink?
follow_up_question: What do you think it would feel like to experience a moonquake?

title: Giant Ice Cube Breaks Free in Antarctica
story: A colossal chunk of ice broke away from Antarctica in 2021, creating the world's largest iceberg. Named A-76, this frozen giant measured a whopping 1,667 square miles. That's bigger than the entire state of Rhode Island! Scientists spotted the massive ice cube on May 13 as it split from the Ronne Ice Shelf. Icebergs like A-76 form naturally as ice shelves grow and shrink in the frigid polar regions.
activity_question: What was the name given to the world's largest iceberg that broke away in 2021?
follow_up_question: Why do you think scientists give icebergs names like A-76?

These daring outlaws roamed the Wild West, robbing trains and causing trouble. Though his life of crime was short-lived, Bob's legacy as a bold bandit lives on in cowboy tales.
activity_question: Where was Bob Dalton born?
follow_up_question: What kind of crimes did the Dalton Gang commit?

title: Inge Lehmann: Earth's Core Detective
story: Inge Lehmann, born in 1888, shook up our understanding of Earth's insides. This Danish scientist used earthquake waves to prove our planet has a solid inner core. Her discovery changed geology forever! Lehmann's brilliant mind and careful observations helped us 'see' deep inside Earth on May 13, her birthday.
activity_question: What did Inge Lehmann discover about Earth?
activity_choices: It has a solid inner core
It has a liquid outer core
activity_answer: It has a solid inner core
follow_up_question: How did Lehmann make her discovery about Earth's core?

title: Sweet Valley High Creator Born
story: Francine Pascal, the creative mind behind the popular 'Sweet Valley High' book series, entered the world in 1932. Born in Manhattan on May 13, she would grow up to captivate young readers with tales of twin sisters Elizabeth and Jessica Wakefield. Pascal's stories of high school drama and adventure became a beloved part of many childhoods.
activity_question: What book series did Francine Pascal create?
follow_up_question: Have you ever read a book from a series? What did you like about it?

title: A Sci-Fi Legend Is Born
story: Imagine a world where magic and technology collide. That's what Roger Zelazny brought to life! Born in 1937 on May 13 in Euclid, Ohio, Zelazny grew up to become a master of science fiction and fantasy. His most famous work, the Chronicles of Amber series, took readers on wild adventures through parallel universes.
activity_question: What was Roger Zelazny's most famous work?
follow_up_question: What do you think a story that mixes magic and technology might be like?

title: The Voice That Shaped Motown
story: A star was born in Detroit on May 13, 1943. Mary Wells, the future 'Queen of Motown,' entered the world with a voice that would change music. She grew up to sing hit songs like 'My Guy' and 'You Beat Me to the Punch.' Wells helped put Motown on the map, paving the way for other great artists.
activity_question: What famous nickname was Mary Wells known by?
bonus_fact: Mary Wells was Motown's first solo star to have a Top 10 pop hit.
